response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist engineer with ~18 years of experience, strong TypeScript/Node.js skills, cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), and recent hands-on work with MCP servers and agentic-workflow tooling — directly relevant to key technologies in this role. However, the role requires 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, and his AI-specific experience appears to span roughly one year. There is no demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, evaluation pipelines, or Python, and customer-facing technical discovery leadership is not evidenced. Worth a conversation given the MCP/agentic overlap and staff-level engineering maturity, but the AI depth gap is significant.
strengths: Current hands-on experience building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks
Deep production engineering background (18+ years) across web, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems
Strong TypeScript/Node.js and cloud infrastructure skills (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker)
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~1 year of GenAI work)
No evidence of RAG, vector database, LLM evaluation, or multi-agent framework (LangGraph/CrewAI) experience
Python is absent from the skill set, and customer-facing technical discovery leadership is not demonstrated

key_factors.factor: Missing RAG/vector DB and Python expertise
